name: Azalea Twilight
description: This tool estimates the short-term inhaled PM2.5 dose for an individual walking in two  distinct urban environments: a busy arterial road and a residential side street.  It assumes a constant concentration (C) and breathing rate (BR) over the duration  of the trip, calculating the total mass of particulate matter inhaled.  The results provide an order-of-magnitude comparison of exposure pathways rather  than a precise health risk assessment or complex atmospheric dispersion model.
